author: tulasī⋅dāsa
verses: ṭhumaka calata rāmacandra bājata paiñjaniyā kilaka kilaka oṭhata dhāe, girata bhūmi laṭapaṭāe dhāya māta goda leta daśaratha ki raniyā vidurama se aruṇa adhara, bolata mukha madhura⋅madhura subhaga nāsikā me cāru laṭakata laṭakaniyā tulasī dāsa ati ānanda, dekhi kai mukhāravinda raghuvara chabi ke samāna raghuvara chabi baniyā
en_translation:
Lord Rāmacandra walks with toddling steps, and His anklets are jingling.

Giggling, He gets up and runs, then falls upon the ground, stumbling. Queen Kaushalyā runs and takes Him into her lap.

His lips are reddish like rubies, and He speaks very sweetly. On His beautiful nose, a charming ornament dangles.

Tulasīdāsa feels great joy upon seeing His lotus-like face. The beauty of the Lord is His only comparison.
